guarantees: Vite + React (JS) frontend scaffold with router-ready pages
Python FastAPI backend sketch and SQL migration stubs
Workspace preview (Sandpack) and compile-style verification gates
build_target_meta.label: Full-stack web (Vite + React)
on_this_run: Primary app lives at repo root (package.json + src/) as today.
roadmap: Optional Turbopack / RSC experiments as a separate track.
build_target_meta.tagline: Default Auto-Runner path — SPA, Sandpack preview, FastAPI sketch, SQL, verifiers.

capability_notice: CrucibAI is aimed at apps, websites, backend services, automation, and agents — not a single narrow niche. Each Auto-Runner job picks an **execution target** for this run’s pipeline; approved jobs run to completion.
This run: Full-stack web (Vite + React) — Default Auto-Runner path — SPA, Sandpack preview, FastAPI sketch, SQL, verifiers.

estimate.note: Final cost depends on complexity encountered during execution.

missing_inputs.description: For live charges add to backend/.env; dev builds can use checkout mocks / placeholders.

acceptance_criteria: All frontend pages load without errors
All API routes return expected response shapes
Requested stack components are emitted as real files across application, services, tests, docs, and infrastructure.
Complex requests route through the full AGENT_DAG swarm instead of pack/scaffold fallback.
Payment flows include server handlers, configuration, and integration notes.
Orchestration brain: keep agent activation relevant to the goal; expand dependencies only when the DAG requires them.
Verify touched scopes at phase boundaries; do not rely on a single late gate for syntax, imports, or types.
Treat failures as checkpointed state: persist progress, classify errors, repair the smallest surface, then re-run targeted checks.

architecture_outline.api_contract_intent: Multi-agent swarm emits API/backend artifacts across requested services.
architecture_outline.auth_flow_intent: Agent swarm should emit auth artifacts requested by the prompt; verify refresh/MFA/SSO explicitly.
architecture_outline.billing_intent: Braintree stubs + idempotency SQL sketch if Braintree integration detected
data_model_intent: Demo items / user preferences (client storage + sketch API)
No automatic tenant_id / RLS unless goal triggers multi_tenant integration

brain_policy.name: crucib_brain_policy
brain_policy.system_directive_text: You are CrucibAI's orchestration brain. Govern execution; do not merely dispatch agents. Accept complex single-prompt builds, decompose internally into dependency-aware phases, spawn only relevant agents, maintain a live context registry, enforce file-type-correct generation, verify continuously, persist all valid progress, repair deterministically, accept mid-run steering, resume from checkpoint, and never mark work complete without proof.

spec_guard.runner_truth: CrucibAI now has two build paths: deterministic packs for proven tracks and a direct full-system builder for complex prompts. The direct builder may emit mixed-language workspaces spanning frontend, backend, data, infra, tests, and docs. Verification depth still varies by stack: the web preview gate is deepest for browser-facing apps, while non-web or multi-service stacks may currently rely more on file/proof verification than full runtime execution. Complex tenancy, compliance, infra, and vendor integrations should still be reviewed explicitly before production.

response.production_readiness_cap_note: Production readiness is capped because the final build/preview was not verified. Passing artifacts, route proof, and live preview are required before readiness can rise.

scorecard.honest_summary: Pipeline quality (proof density): ~40. Trust-weighted evidence: ~78. Spec compliance vs stated goal: ~100%. Production readiness: not eligible. Export/deploy are only safe after the contract, build, preview, and proof gates pass.

spec_guard.runner_truth: CrucibAI now has two build paths: deterministic packs for proven tracks and a direct full-system builder for complex prompts. The direct builder may emit mixed-language workspaces spanning frontend, backend, data, infra, tests, and docs. Verification depth still varies by stack: the web preview gate is deepest for browser-facing apps, while non-web or multi-service stacks may currently rely more on file/proof verification than full runtime execution. Complex tenancy, compliance, infra, and vendor integrations should still be reviewed explicitly before production.
